context:
Section 7 (Conclusions), page 27 (page 27/28 of the parsed text)

Nevertheless, there are many other unresolved stability issues in NEPs. For instance, is the I-property also necessary for the isolated calmness of \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\)? In the context of NLP, the answer is positive, where the I-property actually reduces to the SOSC (Dontchev and Rockafellar [9]) under the SONC for optimality.
title: Necessity of the I-property for isolated calmness of the KKT solution mapping in Nash equilibrium problems
latex:
Let \(N\in\mathbb{N}\) be the number of players. For each player \(k\in\{1,\dots,N\}\), let \(n_k,m_k,s_k,d_k\in\mathbb{N}\) with \(s_k\le m_k\). Let \(f_k:\mathbb{R}^{n}\times\mathbb{R}^{d_k}\to\mathbb{R}\) and \(g^k_i:\mathbb{R}^{n_k}\times\mathbb{R}^{d_k}\to\mathbb{R}\) be \(C^2\) functions for \(i\in\{1,\dots,m_k\}\), where \(n:=\sum_{k=1}^N n_k\). For a parameter \(p=(p_1,\dots,p_N)\) with \(p_k=(u_k,v_k,w_k)\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\times\mathbb{R}^{n_k}\times\mathbb{R}^{d_k}\), define player \(k\)'s perturbed optimization problem
\[
\min_{x_k\in\mathbb{R}^{n_k}}\; f_k(x_k,x_{-k};w_k)-\langle v_k,x_k\rangle
\]
subject to
\[
 g^k_i(x_k;w_k)=u^k_i\ \text{for } i=1,\dots,s_k,\qquad
 g^k_j(x_k;w_k)\le u^k_j\ \text{for } j=s_k+1,\dots,m_k,
\]
where \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_N)\in\mathbb{R}^n\) and \(x_{-k}\) denotes \(x\) without component \(x_k\). A KKT triple at \(p\) is a pair \((x,\lambda)\) with \(\lambda=(\lambda_1,\dots,\lambda_N)\), \(\lambda_k\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\), satisfying for every \(k\):
- stationarity \(v_k=\nabla_{x_k}L_k(x,\lambda_k;w_k)\), where \(L_k(x,\lambda_k;w_k):=f_k(x_k,x_{-k};w_k)+\sum_{i=1}^{m_k}\lambda^k_i g^k_i(x_k;w_k)\);
- primal feasibility \(g^k_i(x_k;w_k)=u^k_i\) for \(i\le s_k\) and \(g^k_j(x_k;w_k)\le u^k_j\) for \(j>s_k\);
- dual feasibility \(\lambda^k_j\ge 0\) for \(j>s_k\);
- complementarity \(\lambda^k_j\,(g^k_j(x_k;w_k)-u^k_j)=0\) for \(j>s_k\).
Let \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) denote the (set-valued) mapping that sends \(p\) to the set of all such \((x,\lambda)\).
Fix \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) with \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\in S_{\mathrm{KKT}}(\bar p)\). For each player \(k\), define the active-constraint index sets at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\)
\[
I^k_1:=\{1,\dots,s_k\}\cup\{j\in\{s_k+1,\dots,m_k\}:\ \bar\lambda^k_j>0\},\qquad
I^k_2:=\{j\in\{s_k+1,\dots,m_k\}:\ \bar\lambda^k_j=0,\ g^k_j(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)=\bar u^k_j\},
\]
and set \(I_1:=\bigcup_{k=1}^N I^k_1\) and \(I_2:=\bigcup_{k=1}^N I^k_2\). Define the product cone
\[
K(I_1,I_2):=\Big\{y=(y_1,\dots,y_N)\in\mathbb{R}^n:\ \nabla_{x_k}g^k_i(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)^\top y_k=0\ \forall i\in I^k_1,\ \nabla_{x_k}g^k_i(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)^\top y_k\le 0\ \forall i\in I^k_2,\ \forall k\Big\}.
\]
Define the block-Hessian terms \(H_{k i}:=\nabla^2_{x_k x_i} L_k(\bar x,\bar\lambda_k;\bar w_k)\in\mathbb{R}^{n_k\times n_i}\) for \(k,i\in\{1,\dots,N\}\). The I-property at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) (on \(K(I_1,I_2)\)) is:
\[
\text{for every } y\in K(I_1,I_2),\ \Big(\sum_{i=1}^N y_k^\top H_{k i} y_i=0\ \text{for all } k\in\{1,\dots,N\}\Big)\ \Longrightarrow\ y=0.
\]
The problem is to determine whether the I-property is necessary for isolated calmness of the KKT solution mapping at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\), i.e., whether the following implication holds under appropriate standard regularity assumptions (e.g., local closedness of \(\mathrm{gph}\,S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) and suitable constraint qualifications ensuring multiplier uniqueness):
\[
S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\ \text{is isolated calm at } \bar p \text{ for } (\bar x,\bar\lambda)\ \Longrightarrow\ \text{the I-property holds at } (\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\text{ on }K(I_1,I_2).
\]
Equivalently, either prove the implication or provide a counterexample NEP (with explicit \(f_k,g^k_i\)) for which \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) is isolated calm at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) but the I-property fails.

In the context of NLP, the answer is positive, where the I-property actually reduces to the SOSC (Dontchev and Rockafellar [9]) under the SONC for optimality.
